description:
Advances: specled.coverage_capture, specled.triangulation, specled.spec_review, specled.decision_governance

.spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md rewrite: aggregate-first intent; new must-requirements aggregate_ingest, versioned_artifact (supersedes the no-in-band-version clause), formatter_disarmed_by_default, line_level_delta_snapshots (per-test lane), cast_time_compaction, degenerate_artifact_rejected; serialized_run rescoped to --per-test and escalated warning->failure; keyed_by_test_pid rewritten to honest {module, name} key (pid claim confirmed fictional); new scenarios: never-executed function ABSENT from records; bare wiring produces no artifact. .spec/specs/triangulation.spec.md: restate untested_realization/execution_reach_metric over the envelope; gate untethered_test/underspecified_realization on mode :per_test; new closure_coverage_metric requirement; Decision 1: fix the cold_run_detector_unavailable scenario's false 'mix spec.check runs triangulation' premise — rescope to spec.triangle/spec.review, spec.check stays fast, NO gate wiring. .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md: coverage_tab_bind_closure restated; degraded states. NEW ADR specled.decision.aggregate_first_spec_coverage.md superseding-in-part serialized_per_test_coverage (serialization rationale retained verbatim for the opt-in lane; the &:cover.analyse/1 default and default-posture claims superseded; codifies headline-ANY-test + self-verified semantics, read-only/never-reset invariant, OTP recommend->=27.2-no-hard-gate matrix, versioned envelope). mix spec.check must pass for all touched subjects.

## Orchestrator addenda (admission, epic @ 3abf5e1)

REALITY CHECK: much of this ticket's originally-planned spec surgery already landed as incremental co-changes (155.1-155.7): store_v2_* requirements, aggregate_ingest, serialized_run rescoped to --per-test, formatter arming/disarm requirements, envelope triangulation requirements, coverage_tab_bind_closure v2 rewrite. Your job is the CONSOLIDATION and the durable ADR, not re-doing landed work:

1. NEW ADR .spec/decisions/specled.decision.aggregate_first_spec_coverage.md — the centerpiece. Supersedes-in-part serialized_per_test_coverage (retain its serialization rationale verbatim for the opt-in lane; supersede the &:cover.analyse/1 default and default-posture claims). Codify: aggregate-first default; headline metric = %% of realization-closure MFAs covered by ANY test; self-verified composite verdict (never a fake per-spec percent); versioned v2 envelope (supersedes any no-in-band-version clause); read-only/never-:cover.reset one-owner invariant (empirically verified); OTP posture per maintainer decision 4 (recommend >= 27.2, no hard gate); legacy artifacts rejected per decision 5 (no auto-migrate); task name kept per decision 2; consumers = spec.triangle + spec.review only per decision 1.
2. AUDIT-CARRIED OBLIGATION (from 155.5 audit, binding): the renderer labels a non-degraded :per_test envelope 'exact per-test attribution', but the engine cannot detect the ExUnit async-cast race (specled_-cpw; ~1/3 exclusive-attribution failures measured; disclosed in Formatter moduledoc + ADR amendment). Reconcile ALL claim surfaces to honest language (approximate/observed attribution, race-bounded): the html.ex/spec.triangle.ex label strings, spec_review.spec.md / triangulation.spec.md / coverage_capture.spec.md statements, and the new ADR. Reference specled_-cpw where the bound is described.
3. Coherence pass over coverage_capture.spec.md / triangulation.spec.md / spec_review.spec.md: retire stale claims the incremental co-changes missed — specifically hunt (a) any keyed_by_test_pid/test_pid-keying claim (root-cause report called the pid claim fictional; verify against current formatter code and fix or confirm already-fixed), (b) any remaining text implying mix spec.check runs triangulation (decision 1: it does not and will not — the cold_run_detector_unavailable scenario premise), (c) scenarios promised by the design that may be missing: never-executed function ABSENT from records; bare test_helper wiring produces NO artifact.
4. Do NOT weaken any requirement that landed with passing verification. Append-only discipline for ADRs (adr_append_only). All spec.check targets for every touched subject must pass.

Spec co-change authorization applies as usual; .spec/realization_hashes.json is tool-managed. A green gate outranks Out of Scope.

LESSONS:
- append new code below existing api_boundary-bound functions (specled_-wp7, from 155.1)
- mix compile --force before spec.check seeds hashes for brand-new bound functions (from 155.6)

## Allowed touches

- .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md, .spec/specs/triangulation.spec.md, .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md, .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md
- .spec/decisions/specled.decision.aggregate_first_spec_coverage.md (new)
- .spec/decisions/specled.decision.serialized_per_test_coverage.md (append-only amendment only, if supersession notice is required there)
- lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex (ONLY label/wording strings for obligation 2 + the test assertions those strings pin)
- test/specled_ex/review/html_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_triangle_test.exs, test/specled_ex/review_test.exs (only assertions pinned to reworded labels)
- .spec/realization_hashes.json (tool-regenerated)

## Out of Scope (files)

- lib/specled_ex/coverage/** and all other lib/** beyond the two label-string files
- test/** beyond the three listed label-assertion files
- docs/** (T9)
- priv/**

## Out of Scope (intent)

- No behavior changes — wording/spec/ADR only
- No requirement weakening beyond honest-language reconciliation (obligation 2)
- Nothing wired into mix spec.check

## Verification

- New ADR exists, passes decision-governance/doc-identifier lint, referenced from the specs it governs
- adr_append_only detector silent across all decision-file changes
- No claim surface (spec statement, moduledoc reference, html label, task output) still says 'exact' per-test attribution; race-bounded language references specled_-cpw
- grep proves no spec text claims spec.check runs triangulation
- keyed_by_test_pid claim verified against current formatter code (fixed or confirmed-fixed)
- Scenarios present: never-executed function absent from records; bare wiring produces no artifact
- Full mix spec.check green (all touched subjects, command execution on); doc-identifier lint green
- Label-pinned tests updated and green; mix compile --warnings-as-errors, format, deps.unlock green

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: impl-1
Log: /Users/mzubieta/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-155.8/codex-impl-1/log

Commit: 9fb56ef
Files touched: .spec/decisions/specled.decision.aggregate_first_spec_coverage.md (new), .spec/decisions/specled.decision.serialized_per_test_coverage.md, .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md, .spec/specs/triangulation.spec.md, .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md, lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex
Tests added/updated: none required — no pinned label text changed (only comments and title/tooltip attributes added); confirmed via full run of test/specled_ex/review/html_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_triangle_test.exs, test/specled_ex/review_test.exs (133 tests, 0 failures) that existing pinned assertions still hold.
Verification stubs flipped: none (no `execute: false` stubs in scope for this ticket)

Claim surfaces reconciled (obligation 2 — audit-carried "exact per-test attribution" overclaim, specled_-cpw):
- .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md Intent paragraph (per-test flag description) + `specled.coverage_capture.keyed_by_test_pid` requirement: rewritten to state the actual {module, name} fallback key (test_pid only via opt-in tag) and disclose that key uniqueness does not close the ExUnit test_finished cast-timing race; references specled_-cpw.
- .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md `specled.spec_review.coverage_tab_bind_closure` requirement: "executed" strength / per_test closure_coverage_pct reframed as observed/race-bounded, never exact, independent of the `degraded` flag; references specled_-cpw.
- lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex:2972-3020ish — render_proxy_note/1 and render_reached_by_tests_row/2 comments rewritten off "exact per-test attribution" language; added a `title` tooltip (@reached_by_tests_title) to the "Reached by tests" label disclosing the race bound, without altering any pinned visible label text.
- New ADR specled.decision.aggregate_first_spec_coverage.md: dedicated "Per-test attribution is race-bounded, not exact (specled_-cpw)" section is the durable home for this disclosure.
- Audited lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex: no "exact" claim found in any rendered/label string (confirmed via grep + moduledoc read) — no change needed there.

Coherence-pass findings (obligation 3):
(a) keyed_by_test_pid — CONFIRMED STALE/FICTIONAL against lib/specled_ex/coverage/formatter.ex's extract_key/1 (defaults to {module, name}; test_pid only via @tag test_pid opt-in, since ExUnit does not expose runtime pid in test.tags). Fixed in coverage_capture.spec.md (see above); verified formatter_test.exs already tests the real fallback behavior correctly, only the spec statement was wrong.
(b) "mix spec.check runs triangulation" — CONFIRMED STALE. triangulation.spec.md's `cold_run_detector_unavailable` scenario's `when:` clause said "mix spec.check runs with triangulation enabled" (never true; Decision 1 rescoped consumers to spec.triangle/spec.review only, already reflected correctly in the subject's Intent/summary text). Fixed the scenario's premise; the underlying tests (test/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation_test.exs) already call CoverageTriangulation.findings/3 directly and never claimed spec.check involvement, so no test changes needed.
(c) Missing scenarios — both CONFIRMED ALREADY PRESENT, not missing: "bare wiring produces no artifact" = specled.coverage_capture.scenario.formatter_disarmed_by_default (coverage_capture.spec.md); "never-executed function ABSENT from records" = jointly covered by scenario.formatter_no_fabrication + scenario.snapshot_diff_strictly_increased (per-test: unchanged/never-hit lines never appear in a test's record, no placeholder) and scenario.aggregate_ingest_child_beam (aggregate mode: test/specled_ex/coverage/aggregate_test.exs:66-67 asserts a never-called function appears in :mfas as covered:false, never fabricated as covered or omitted).

Verification tier: change-scoped — mix compile --warnings-as-errors (clean), mix compile --force + full `mix spec.check` (command execution on) run 3x: run 1 hit a pre-existing tempfile/parallelism flake in store_test.exs unrelated to this diff (passed standalone, same class as the epic's documented specled_-epl tempfile-race advisory from MERGE GATE 1); runs 2 and 3 both green (status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, exit 0, 1 pre-existing info finding on lib/specled_ex/coverage.ex unrelated to this diff). Targeted: mix test on the three label-assertion files (133 tests, 0 failures) + test/specled_ex/coverage/, coverage_triangulation_test.exs, spec_cover_test_test.exs, spec_cover_ingest_test.exs, coverage_closure_test.exs (124 tests, 0 failures) + test/docs_identifier_lint_test.exs (2 tests, 0 failures). mix format --check-formatted clean. Full suite / PR CI is the merge gate's responsibility, not re-run here.
Final-tier runs: 3 (1 flake unrelated to diff, 2 clean)

Deferred must requirements: none omitted from this ticket's Allowed touches. Filed specled_-jjq (standalone, not epic-child) for a pre-existing gap surfaced while updating the file-level-proxy wording: CoverageClosure's :per_test clause (lib/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure.ex, out of this ticket's Allowed touches) was never wired to the real per-test MFA data specled_-155.5 shipped — it still uses the file-level proxy the 155.7-era code comment said was temporary. Not a requirement of this ticket; documented so the stale "pending" framing I corrected in html.ex doesn't quietly become a new stale claim.

Epic lessons attestation: (1) append-below-bound-functions — verified html.ex carries zero api_boundary realized_by bindings anywhere in .spec/specs/*.spec.md (grepped), so this lesson does not apply to my in-body edits there; no other touched file has api_boundary bindings either. (2) mix compile --force before spec.check — ran before the final-tier full spec.check.

Summary: Authored the epic's centerpiece ADR (aggregate-first default, ANY-test closure metric, self-verified composite, versioned v2 envelope, read-only invariant, OTP posture, legacy-artifact rejection, task-name/consumer-scope decisions) superseding-in-part specled.decision.serialized_per_test_coverage with an append-only cross-reference amendment there. Reconciled the audit-carried "exact per-test attribution" overclaim to honest, specled_-cpw-referencing language across two specs, the new ADR, and html.ex's comments/tooltips. Ran a coherence pass over all three touched subject specs confirming two stale claims (keyed_by_test_pid, spec.check-runs-triangulation) and fixing both, and confirming two "missing" scenarios were in fact already covered under different names.

title: T8: Spec + ADR edits (coverage_capture, triangulation, spec_review; new aggregate-first ADR)
